items.briefing:
Australia has updated its cybersecurity baseline framework, replacing the long-standing Essential Eight with a new "Essentials" standard — Australian organisations should check whether their security practices need to align with the updated requirements. Separately, research shows overall cybercrime risk for Australians is declining, but small and medium businesses are increasingly being targeted as larger organisations improve their defences, so SMBs should not become complacent.

Globally, critical software vulnerabilities are being discovered at twice the previous rate, meaning unpatched systems are at greater risk than ever. **Practical tip:** Check with your IT team or provider that all software and devices are up to date — especially if your organisation hasn't reviewed its patch status recently.

featured.title: Australia replaces Essential Eight with new Essentials
featured.quote: Australia's cyber baseline shift from Essential Eight to new Essentials reflects critical regulatory evolution for all businesses.
